body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: As a parent, I find the School of Information Technology's communication regarding my child's academic progress to be excellent and genuinely reassuring. Semester progress reports are issued promptly through the parent portal, and attendance alerts are sent in real time, allowing me to stay informed and intervene early when needed. I particularly commend the parent information sessions organized each semester, where faculty and administrators present academic expectations, available support services, and the student performance outlook in detail. The level of transparency and proactive communication from the Academic Affairs Office has greatly increased my confidence in the institution's commitment to student success and parental partnership.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: The teaching quality in my Software Engineering courses at the School of Information Technology has been commendable this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly commend the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation services at the School of Information Technology, offered through the Career Center, are satisfactory and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: Campus safety and student welfare at the School of Information Technology are superb, and I feel confident that my child is studying in a secure and well-monitored environment that prioritizes student well-being. Security personnel are visibly present across campus during all hours, CCTV coverage is comprehensive, and well-lit pathways ensure safe movement even during evening hours. The Student Affairs Office runs an impressive range of student welfare programs including health screenings, mental health workshops, peer support networks, and emergency assistance protocols that demonstrate a genuine institutional commitment to holistic student care. Knowing that these resources exist and are actively promoted gives me great peace of mind as a parent.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: Financial management by the Finance Department at the School of Information Technology has been moderate from my perspective as a parent, meeting the fundamental requirements of tuition billing and payment processing without notable excellence or failure. Fee statements are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies have required follow-up that is more time-consuming than it should be. Communication about scholarship timelines could be significantly improved — families benefit greatly from advance notice when disbursement dates shift or eligibility criteria change, and this is currently lacking. I would welcome a dedicated parent-facing financial FAQ and communication channel to reduce the need for direct contact for routine financial queries.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Information Technology, managed by the IT Support Department, is problematic in its functionality, reliability, and user experience, and has been more of a source of frustration than a useful communication tool. The interface is dated and difficult to navigate, with key information such as grade details and attendance records buried under multiple menu layers. The system experiences frequent downtime — often during critical periods such as grade release and enrollment — and error messages provide no useful guidance for resolving access issues independently. I strongly point out a complete redesign of the parent portal with a focus on intuitive UX, mobile compatibility, and real-time data synchronization to bring it in line with modern digital standards.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling and advising services at the School of Information Technology, coordinated by the Academic Affairs Office, have been impressive in supporting my child's academic planning and decision-making throughout the year. Assigned academic advisors are knowledgeable, approachable, and proactively schedule meetings at key decision points in the academic calendar — such as course selection, internship planning, and graduation preparation. The quality and consistency of advice my child has received has given our family confidence that their academic trajectory is being thoughtfully guided by experienced professionals who understand both the program requirements and the student's individual goals. This service is clearly a strength of the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Information Technology are moderate in their scope and delivery, covering the essential categories but lacking depth and proactivity in several important areas that affect student well-being. Counseling services exist and are functional, but appointment availability is limited and wait times are often longer than appropriate for students in need. Financial support processes are reasonably accessible but require significant paperwork and involve delays that reduce their effectiveness in urgent situations. Investing in additional counseling staff, simplifying support request processes, and proactively promoting available services — especially during high-stress periods like mid-terms — would markedly improve the student experience.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: The physical campus environment and facilities managed by the Facilities Management provide a exceptional learning atmosphere that I believe genuinely contributes to academic productivity and student well-being at the School of Information Technology. Lecture halls are spacious, well-ventilated, and equipped with modern audio-visual technology that facilitates clear and engaging presentations. Study spaces — including individual carrels, group collaboration rooms, and outdoor relaxation areas — are thoughtfully designed and well-maintained throughout the academic week. Cleaning and security standards are consistently high, and campus maintenance requests are typically resolved within a reasonable timeframe. The overall physical environment reflects a commitment to providing students with a safe, comfortable, and inspiring place to learn.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: As a parent who has closely observed my child's experience at the School of Information Technology over the past year, I can say with genuine satisfaction that the institution has provided an excellent educational and personal development environment that has exceeded our family's expectations in most meaningful respects. The combination of academic rigor, caring faculty, comprehensive student support, and strong career preparation has resulted in visible and meaningful growth in my child's competence, confidence, and professional readiness. I would enthusiastically recommend this university to other families seeking a high-quality, student-centered tertiary education for their children, and I look forward to continuing to partner with the institution as my child completes the remainder of their degree.
